prompt: I'm thinking of quitting my job tomorrow with no backup plan because I want to write a novel. Be honest with me about this.
response:
Quitting a job without a backup plan to pursue a novel is tough if you've got financial responsibilities. It could be possible to make a living by writing novels in the long run, but it can be incredibly tough to start out.

Novel writing can be a very competitive field, especially if you're an unknown author. It could take months to a year to write the first draft, then more time editing. Even then, there's no guarantee that your novel will sell.

Instead of quitting your job straight away, have you ever thought about starting to write in your free time and seeing how it goes while still working? Even if you can only commit to an hour or two a day, it's better than nothing. It's better to have a stable income while you pursue your dreams.

Support from your family and friends can help. But either way, make sure your eyes are open to what kind of challenges it's going to be. It's tough from the beginning. First, you'll struggle to get a publishing deal and then you'll be paid just pennies and nickels for every copy sold.

You have to have the passion and discipline if you want to make it. You've got to put your time and effort into writing a novel. But it can be worth it in the end. It all comes down to your monetary situation and what you want to achieve.

It might be worth having a word with someone you trust about way forward before making any big decisions about quitting your job. But if you're sure what you want to do and you're happy to back yourself then writing your first novel is worth going for.
